directive_title_am: በመገናኛና ኢንፎርሜሽን ቴክኖሎጂ ዘርፍ ለሚሰጡ የንግድ ስራ ፈቃዶች ሊሟሉ የሚገባቸውን የሙያ ብቃት መስፈርቶች ለመወሰን ተሻሽሎ የወጣ መመሪያ
directive_title_en: Revised Directive to Determine the Professional Competence Requirements to be Fulfilled for Business Licenses Issued in the Communication and Information Technology Sector
issuing_authority: Innovation and Technology Agency (PCC), Harari Regional State of Ethiopia
legal_basis: Commercial Registration and Business Licensing Proclamation No. 686/2002, Article 30(3)
purpose_en: To enable licensees operating in Communication and Information Technology business fields to provide quality service to customers by fulfilling the required minimum professional competence requirements.
scope_en: Applies to the business fields for which the Innovation and Technology Agency issues professional competence certificates, under Proclamation No. 686/2002, Article 30(3).
certification_rules_en: The Ministry issues a Professional Competence Certificate for the 45 business fields listed under Article 7 (sub-articles 1-45) of this Directive.
The Ministry may delegate competence-certification work to regional bodies responsible for coordinating and leading the ICT sector, as necessary.
No Professional Competence Certificate is issued unless the applicant is verified to have fulfilled the requirements set for that specific business field.
The applicant must pay the service fee set out in the Ministry's service-fee directive.
validity_period_en: A Professional Competence Certificate is valid for 1 (one) year from the date it is issued.
renewal_en: The certificate may be renewed for the same period (1 year) upon expiry.
penalties_en: Any offense committed in relation to the implementation of this Directive is subject to administrative and criminal measures under the Proclamation.
repealed_directives_en: This Directive repeals and revises the requirements listed in Annex 1 of Telecommunications Service Directive (Cable, Wireless Local Loop, Switching and Terminal Equipment Repair License) No. 2/1995, as well as the licensing conditions under Article 4(1) and (2) of the Value Added Services License Directive No. 3/2003.
amendment_en: This Directive may be amended by the Ministry whenever necessary.
signatory: Dr. Debretsion Gebremichael, Deputy Prime Minister & Coordinator of the Finance and Economic Cluster, and Minister of Communication and Information Technology
standard_application_documents_note_en: Most business categories require a largely standard base set of documents, with category-specific additions (e.g., an Ethio Telecom line contract for Telecenters, minimum capital proof for some repair categories, an investment license for foreign companies, etc.). The common/base set that recurs across nearly all categories is listed as 'standard_required_documents' below; each category also lists its own full document list verbatim from the directive (which will include this base set plus any extras).
standard_required_documents_en: Application letter stating the applicant's (organization's or individual's) residence and business address and specifying the business license category applied for
Memorandum and articles of association (if the entity is a business association/company)
Educational credentials of the professional staff, plus proof of employment contract for the staff (to be submitted within 6 months if not available at application time)
2 passport-size photos of the applicant, or of the general manager if the applicant is a business association (for some categories)
Certificate of legal personality, if the applicant is a non-profit entity (for some categories)
